title: Electron Microscopy with Primary Hippocampal Neurons
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ofjza","text":"This protocol provides a reliable method for high-resolution electron microscopy of primary cultured hippocampal neurons, allowing detailed visualization of lipid droplets at synaptic terminals.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Isolate and dissociate hippocampal neurons using standard dissection techniques.
Plate 38,000 neurons within 5 mm cylinders mounted on poly-ornithine coated glass coverslips.
Maintain neurons for 14-21 days under standard culture conditions ( 37 °C , 5% CO 2 ) until ready for fixation.
Prepare a fixative solution containing 2.5% glutaraldehyde and 2 millimolar (mM) CaCl 2 in 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer.
Fix the neurons by gently applying the fixative solution for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Wash the fixed neurons 4 times with 0.1 M sodium cacodylate buffer containing 2 mM CaCl 2 , 5 minutes per wash.
Pre-incubate the cells in 0.1 Molarity (M) imidazole buffer for 00:05:00 .
Fix the cells in 2% Osmium in 0.1 Molarity (M) imidazole buffer for 00:30:00 at Room temperature .
Wash the cells in water 5 minutes x 4.
En bloc stain the neurons using 2% aqueous uranyl acetate for 01:00:00 at Room temperature or 4 °C Overnight .
Allow the samples to stain thoroughly to enhance contrast for electron microscopy.
Wash the cells in water 5 minutes x4.
Embed the neurons in Embed 812 resin according to the manufacturer’s instructions.
Use an ultramicrotome (Leica) to cut ultrathin sections (50-60 nm thick) from the embedded samples.
Mount sections onto grids suitable for transmission electron microscopy.
Place the sections in a Talos L 120C TEM microscope.
Operate the microscope at 80 kV to capture high-resolution images.

Use Velox software and a 4k × 4k Ceta CMOS camera for imaging. All reagents for electron microscopy were acquired from EMS (Electron Microscopy Sciences), Hatfield, PA. Handle all chemicals, especially OsO 4 and uranyl acetate, with care and in a fume hood to prevent exposure to toxic fumes. Ultrathin sectioning requires practice; ensure sections are even and smooth for optimal imaging.
Wash the fixed neurons with 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer containing 2 millimolar (mM) CaCl 2 , 00:05:00 per wash. (1/4)
Wash the fixed neurons with 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer containing 2 millimolar (mM) CaCl 2 , 00:05:00 per wash. (2/4)
Wash the fixed neurons with 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer containing 2 millimolar (mM) CaCl 2 , 00:05:00 per wash. (3/4)
Wash the fixed neurons with 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer containing 2 millimolar (mM) CaCl 2 , 00:05:00 per wash. (4/4)
Wash the cells in water 00:05:00 . (1/4)
Wash the cells in water 00:05:00 . (2/4)
Wash the cells in water 00:05:00 . (3/4)
Wash the cells in water 00:05:00 . (4/4)
Wash the cells in water 00:05:00 . (1/4)
Wash the cells in water 00:05:00 . (2/4)
Wash the cells in water 00:05:00 . (3/4)
Wash the cells in water 00:05:00 . (4/4)
Infiltrate the cells in 50% of Embed 812 in EtoH for 01:00:00 then pure Embed 812 1 hour x2.
Dehydrate the neurons through a graded ethanol series to prepare them for resin embedding (50%, 75%, 95% EtoH for 00:05:00 , 100% EtoH for 10 minutes x3).
Dehydrate in 100% EtoH for 10 minutes. (1/3)
Dehydrate in 100% EtoH for 10 minutes. (2/3)
Dehydrate in 100% EtoH for 10 minutes. (3/3)
